components.evidence: The growth medium, which was pH-adjusted (pH 7.0) and filter-sterilized (0.22 um nylon ...), contained the following major salts: 37 mM NH4Cl, 0.80 mM MgSO4, 0.034 mM CaCl2.2H2O, 20 mM K2HPO4 and 5.0 mM NaH2PO4.

components.name: ferrous (Fe2+) salt
components.concentration: ~650 nM exogenous (38 nM unchelated in Fe-limited condition)
components.evidence: The starting exogenous Fe concentration (~650 nM), provided as Fe2+ salts, was kept the same in all conditions. ... or Fe-limited with 38 nM total dissolved unchelated Fe.

components.name: disodium EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetate)
components.evidence: Disodium ethylene-diamine-tetra-acetic-acid (EDTA) was added as a Fe chelator and the EDTA concentration to be used was determined using the software Visual MINTEQ ... Severe Fe-limited conditions (90 uM EDTA added).

components.name: D-glucose
components.concentration: 16.67 mM (100 mM C-equivalent); 50 mM in 300 mM C runs
components.evidence: Total carbon-equivalent substrate concentrations (100 mM C or 300 mM C total) ... For the single-substrate scenarios, we used glucose (16.67 mM or 50 mM) ... for the severe Fe-limitation, we performed experiments with glucose alone.

media.base_medium: Custom defined minimal (phosphate-buffered) medium with defined trace-metal mix; pH 7.0, filter-sterilized. No cited external base-medium reference; full composition given in this paper's SI Appendix.

sources_chased.what: SI Appendix PDF (Detailed Methods 'Culturing and nutrient conditions' + Table S1 Fe speciation)
sources_chased.how: EuropePMC REST supplementaryFiles zip for PMC7768705 (PNAS/PMC direct download blocked by 403 / proof-of-work gate)

missing_before: phosphate buffer (20 mM K2HPO4 + 5.0 mM NaH2PO4)
media.notes: Complete recipe recovered from SI Appendix (Detailed Methods + Table S1). This is a fully defined phosphate-buffered minimal medium: NH4Cl (N), K2HPO4/NaH2PO4 (P/buffer), MgSO4, CaCl2, and a defined 6-salt trace-metal mix (B, Cu, Zn, Mn, Ni, Mo), with ferrous salt as Fe source and EDTA as chelator to set unchelated Fe (Fe-replete 30 uM / intermediate 650 nM / severe Fe-limited 38 nM; 90 uM EDTA added for the severe condition modeled here). No vitamins and no reductant are used (prototrophic Pseudomonas grown aerobically in baffled flasks with sponge caps, 220 rpm, 30C). Correction: the current record's cobalt (EX_cobalt2_e) is NOT in the true recipe - the medium contains nickel and molybdenum, not cobalt; iron is added only as ferrous salt (the Fe3+/generic Fe exchanges are model artifacts). Citrate appears only in the separate glucose:citrate scenario, not in this glucose-as-sole-carbon medium.
